issues.searchText: Dear Mekor Habracha Community, Remember to join Rabbi Gotlib this week for his new class exploring "The Sacred and the State: The Religious Zionism of Rabbi Dr. Nachum Rabinovitch." Zionism and the establishment of the State of Israel challenge Torah scholars to articulate a vision of state and society that embodies the highest standards of security, sanctity, and justice. Rav Rabinovitch spoke of these issues with a rare sensitivity and wisdom. Join Rabbi Gotlib for an in-depth exploration of Rav Rabinovitch's "Pathways to God: Torah, Society, and State," a book that emphasizes the Torah's "vision of liberty, equality, rights, and responsibilities, which relates closely to modern democratic ideals but is not identical to them." Sponsorship for this class is available at any amount via venmo (@Mekor-Habracha) or donated to the shul here. Sponsors of $180 or more will receive a complimentary copy of Rav Rabinovitch's book. Class will meet Tuesday evenings at 7:00pm beginning tomorrow, July 14th. RSVP HERE! Sincerely, The Mekor Team Copyright © 2026 Mekor Habracha, All rights reserved. Our mailing address is: 1500 Walnut St. Suite 206 Philadelphia, PA 19102 Want to change how you receive these emails? You can update your preferences or unsubscribe from this list

issues.title: Mekor Habracha Newsletter - Matot-Masei / Mevarchim Av
issues.searchText: Parshat Matot - Masei / Mevarchim Av July 10 - 11, 2026 26 Tammuz 5786 Friday, July 10 7:00pm Mincha / Kabbalat Shabbat / Maariv 8:12pm Candle Lighting Shabbat, July 11 9:15am Morning Services 10:00am Torah Reading Thank you to our readers, Yitzchak Tollinsky, Jacob Zieper, Yaakov Hagler, and Yogi Sragow ~11:30am Kiddush 7:00pm Pre-Mincha Classes 8:00pm Mincha / Third Meal / Maariv 9:19pm Havdalah The Center City Eruv is UP! Please help the Center City Eruv with a donation. And, if you're able, the best way to support the Eruv is to become an eruv-checker. Speak to Rabbi Hirsch or Rabbi Gotlib if you are interested in this opportunity! For more details on all Shabbat Zmanim, weekly services, sponsorship opportunities, Mishebeirach requests, and programming, please visit mekorhabracha.org. ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ Kiddush This Week This week's Kiddush is sponsored by Liam and Renee Shamir in honor of the birth of their daughter Ronit. Mazal tov! This Week's Kiddush is also sponsored by the Shamir family as a thank you to the Mekor Community for their help after Ronit's birth. Thank you! This Week's Kiddush is also sponsored by Ellen Geller and Sharon Geller in memory of their uncle, Harold Feder (Chaim Yehoshua ben Yosef), and their mother, Dorothy Feder Geller (Devorah bat Yosef), whose yahrzeits fall on 23 and 24 Tammuz this week. May their memories be a blessing. This week's Kiddush contains MnMs in honor of parshat Matot-Masei sponsored by an anonymous appreciator of cities of refuge. Thank You! Last week's kiddush was sponsored by Rachel Glyn, in honor of July 4th and America 250. Thank you! June 13th's Kiddush was sponsored by Barry and Marla Goldberg and Jim & Beth Plotkin in celebration of Jason and Shayna's Wedding. Mazal tov! We greatly appreciate all of the contributions and gestures, great and small, that make Mekor the inclusive and welcoming community that we are. We are especially thankful to those who sponsor, set up, and clean up our Shabbat dinners, kiddush, and third meal. Thank you for all of your help! ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ Volunteers Needed for Eruv Campaign! We're getting ready to launch an important fundraising campaign for the Center City Eruv this summer, and we're looking for volunteers to help make it a success. Whether you can help reach out to friends, make phone calls, or assist in other ways, we'd greatly appreciate your involvement. Even a small amount of time can make a real difference. If you're interested in helping, please contact Rabbi Hirsch (rabbiehirsch@mekorhabracha.org) or Rabbi Gotlib (rabbisgotlib@mekorhabracha.org). Thank you for helping strengthen this vital community mitzvah! ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ Mekor Membership Join Mekor or Renew Your Membership HERE ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ Tisha B'Av @Mekor ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ New Religious Zionism Class with Rabbi Gotlib! Join Rabbi Gotlib for a study of Rav Rabinovitch's Pathways to God: Torah, Society, and State, exploring the Torah's vision of the Jewish state and its relationship to modern democratic ideals. Sponsorships are welcome at any amount via Venmo (@Mekor-Habracha) or by donation to the shul. Sponsors of $180+ will receive a complimentary copy of the book. Class will meet Tuesday evenings at 7:00pm beginning July 14th. REGISTER HERE ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ Yizkor Books 2026-2027 Submission Deadline: September 1, 2026 @12:00pm It will include Yizkor prayers and a special section dedicated to family and friends whom you wish to memorialize. Mekor will again publish our Yizkor Book for use on Yom Kippur, Shemini Atzeret, Pesach, and Shavuot. Submit name(s) to remember here. You can also contribute towards the Yizkor Books by a check made payable to the shul. ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ Hebrew Help at Mekor Parents can now sign-up their children (up to age 18) to learn Hebrew, Torah Reading, and/or Tefillah skills with members of the Mekor community. Our team will match each child with a tutor who will begin learning with them at a mutually convenient time at the end of the summer/beginning of the upcoming school year. Sign-up HERE! ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ For Kosher Wine use this link. For Judaica use this link. ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ Donations to Mekor We deeply appreciate donations -- large and small -- to help support our shul. To make your contribution, please use this link. ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ Support of Israel: Events & Initiatives In addition to the information below, please visit our new Support of Israel website page for announcements about initiatives online and across the greater Philadelphia Jewish community, all in support of Israel. See our newest entries about how you can support civil defense efforts in Israel. ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ To view the latest prayer for the welfare of soldiers of the IDF and all victims of terror click here. ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ Click here to read Rabbi Hirsch's latest Torah articles on Substack, many of which speak about Israel. Click here to read Rabbi Gotlib's latest articles on Substack. Sunnie Epstein's weekly Divrei Torah on the Parshiot HaShavuah can be found on YouTube here. Daily Minyan We welcome everyone to join us for a Minyan, whether it's once a week or every weekday! Please email the Shul to RSVP for a particular service or to be added to the Minyan WhatsApp group. RSVPs for minyanim are appreciated. Morning and Evening weekday services are followed by a brief halacha insight by Rabbi Hirsch or Rabbi Gotlib. Weekday Services & Events: July 14 - July 17 Sunday, July 14 8:30am Morning Services followed by Amud Yomi 8:00pm Mincha/Maariv* Monday July 15 6:45am Morning Services 7:30am Amud Yomi 2:00pm Mincha at 1818 Market or Mikveh Israel 8:00pm Mincha/Maariv* Tuesday, July 16 6:55am Morning Services 7:30am Amud Yomi 2:00pm Mincha at 1818 Market or Mikveh Israel 8:00pm Mincha/Maariv* Wednesday, July 17 (Rosh Chodesh Av) 6:45am Morning Services 7:30am Amud Yomi 2:00pm Mincha at 1818 Market or Mikveh Israel 8:00pm Mincha Thursday, July 18 6:45am Morning Services 7:30am Amud Yomi 8:00pm Mincha/Maariv* Friday, July 19 6:55am Morning Services 7:30am Amud Yomi 7:00pm Mincha/Kabbalat Shabbat/Maariv 8:09pm Candle Lighting *At this time, Weekday Mincha/Ma'ariv will only be held if at least 10 people sign up in advance. Please email the shul to RSVP for a particular service or to be added to our Minyan WhatsApp Group. Please note that Friday Night Services will remain at 7:00pm through the Summer. We all benefit from the wonderful work done by Mekor volunteers! Please consider helping out with the following initiatives: Join the Kiddush (setup) Committee Join the Hospitality Committee Make cholent for Kiddush. Erik will teach you how to make the special Mekor Habracha recipe! Please contact Erik Schneiman @ erik.schneiman@gmail.com Be a Mashgiach for IKC establishments Be an Eruv Checker Training will be provided for all positions. Please email the Shul for info about volunteering! Like what you see? Subscribe to our weekly newsletter! There is also an excessive heat warning in the city of Philadelphia, with dehydration being a serious risk. If you or loved ones have any questions about who should fast, please reach out to Rabbi Hirsch or Rabbi Gotlib. This fast is rabbinic in nature, with significant room for leniency if needed. issues.title: Eruv Update
issues.searchText: Dear Community, Due to an unexpected issue, the Center City Eruv is currently down. We are actively working to complete the necessary repairs and are optimistic that the eruv will be restored before Shabbat. However, until the repairs are completed and confirmed, please be prepared for the possibility that the eruv may not be operational this Shabbat. We will send another update as soon as we have more information. Thank you for your patience and understanding. issues.preview: Dear Mekor Habracha Community, Remember to join us at 7:00pm tonight, Wednesday, June 17th for a wine tasting and a conversation with Yaakov Zimmerman. Yaakov grew up in Passaic and made aliyah to Israel, where he bui…
issues.searchText: Dear Mekor Habracha Community, Remember to join us at 7:00pm tonight, Wednesday, June 17th for a wine tasting and a conversation with Yaakov Zimmerman. Yaakov grew up in Passaic and made aliyah to Israel, where he built his life around Zionism, leadership, and service. He served as a commander in the elite IDF Paratroopers Division 98 and has completed 800+ days of reserve duty since October 7th, participating in some of Israel’s most critical front-line operations. He speaks about making aliyah, leadership and sacrifice through IDF service, firsthand experiences from Gaza/Lebanon/Syria, reserve duty during wartime, building Jewish life in Shiloh and Aish Kodesh, and faith, resilience, and supporting Israel through action. The event includes a wine tasting featuring wines from his Shiloh Winery alongside conversation and Q&A. Donations towards this event can be made via Venmo (@Mekor-Habracha) or given to the shul here. RSVP HERE! Sincerely, The Mekor Team Copyright © 2026 Mekor Habracha, All rights reserved. He served as a commander in the elite IDF Paratroopers Division 98 and has completed 800+ days of reserve duty since October 7th, participating in some of Israel’s most critical front-line operations. He speaks about making aliyah, leadership and sacrifice through IDF service, firsthand experiences from Gaza/Lebanon/Syria, reserve duty during wartime, building Jewish life in Shiloh and Aish Kodesh, and faith, resilience, and supporting Israel through action. The event includes a wine tasting featuring wines from his Shiloh Winery alongside conversation and Q&A. Donations towards this event can be made via Venmo (@Mekor-Habracha) or given to the shul here. RSVP HERE! Sincerely, The Mekor Team Copyright © 2026 Mekor Habracha, All rights reserved. Our mailing address is: 1500 Walnut St. Suite 206 Philadelphia, PA 19102 Want to change how you receive these emails? You can update your preferences or unsubscribe from this list

issues.searchText: Parshat Shelach / Mevarchim Tammuz June 12-13, 2026 28 Sivan 5786 Friday, June 12 7:00pm Mincha / Kabbalat Shabbat / Maariv 8:11pm Candle Lighting Shabbat, June 13 9:15am Morning Services 10:00am Torah Reading Thank you to our reader, Yaakov Hagler, Yitzchak Tollinsky, Aaron Singer, and Jacob Zieper ~11am Tot Shabbat ~11:30am Kiddush 7:15pm Pre-Mincha Classes 8:15pm Mincha / Third Meal / Maariv 9:20pm Havdala The Center City Eruv is UP! Please help the Center City Eruv with a donation. And, if you're able, the best way to support the Eruv is to become an eruv-checker. Speak to Rabbi Hirsch or Rabbi Gotlib if you are interested in this opportunity! For more details on all Shabbat Zmanim, weekly services, sponsorship opportunities, Mishebeirach requests, and programming, please visit mekorhabracha.org. ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ Third Meal This Week This Week's Third Meal is sponsored by Shaul Kushinsky, Rebecca Somach, Naomi, and Aron in honor of their last Shabbat in Philadelphia with major gratitude to the entire Philadelphia Jewish community- l’hitraot! We will miss you dearly! Kiddush LAST Week Last Week's Kiddush was sponsored by Bruce Taubman commemorating the yahrzeit of his mother, Musa bat Baruch Moshe. May her Neshamah have an aliyah. We greatly appreciate all of the contributions and gestures, great and small, that make Mekor the inclusive and welcoming community that we are. We are especially thankful to those who sponsor, set up, and clean up our Shabbat dinners, kiddush, and third meal. Thank you for all of your help! ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ Mekor Mazal Tovs Mazal tov to Jason and Shayna Goldberg on their wedding this week! ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ Mekor Membership Join Mekor or Renew Your Membership HERE ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ JkidPhilly Grandparent-Child Challah Bake Jkidphilly Grandparents invite you to make challah with us on June 14th in Center City! Each grandchild will have challah dough to knead, flavor and braid. The participants will be bring the completed braided home to bake (or freeze). Location will be provided after you register. RSVP HERE! ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ Yizkor Books 2026-2027 Mekor will again publish our Yizkor Book for use on Yom Kippur, Shemini Atzeret, Pesach, and Shavuot. It will include Yizkor prayers and a special section dedicated to family and friends whom you wish to memorialize. Submission Deadline: September 1, 2026 @12:00pm Contribute to the book here. ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ Wine Tasting with Yaakov Zimmerman RSVP HERE! ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ Hebrew Help at Mekor Parents can now sign-up their children (up to age 18) to learn Hebrew, Torah Reading, and/or Tefillah skills with members of the Mekor community. Our team will match each child with a tutor who will begin learning with them at a mutually convenient time at the end of the summer/beginning of the upcoming school year. Sign-up HERE! ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ For KosherWINE use this link. For Judaica use this link. ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ Donations to Mekor We deeply appreciate donations -- large and small -- to help support our shul. To make your contribution, please use this link. ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ Support of Israel: Events & Initiatives In addition to the information below, please visit our new Support of Israel website page for announcements about initiatives online and across the greater Philadelphia Jewish community, all in support of Israel. See our newest entries about how you can support civil defense efforts in Israel. ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ To view the latest prayer for the welfare of soldiers of the IDF and all victims of terror click here. ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ Click here to read Rabbi Hirsch's latest Torah articles on Substack, many of which speak about Israel. Click here to read Rabbi Gotlib's latest articles on Substack. Sunnie Epstein's weekly Divrei Torah on the Parshiot HaShavuah can be found on YouTube here. Daily Minyan We welcome everyone to join us for a Minyan, whether it's once a week or every weekday! Please email the Shul to RSVP for a particular service or to be added to the Signal Minyan group. RSVPs for minyanim are appreciated. Morning and Evening weekday services are followed by a brief halacha insight by Rabbi Hirsch or Rabbi Gotlib. Weekday Services & Events: June 14 - June 19 Sunday, June 14 8:30am Morning Services followed by Amud Yomi 8:15pm Mincha/Maariv* Monday June 15 (Rosh Chodesh Tammuz) 6:45am Morning Services 7:30am Amud Yomi 2:00pm Mincha at 1818 Market or Mikveh Israel 8:15pm Mincha/Maariv* Tuesday, June 16 (Rosh Chodesh Tammuz Day 2) 6:55am Morning Services 7:30am Amud Yomi 2:00pm Mincha at 1818 Market or Mikveh Israel 8:15pm Mincha/Maariv* Wednesday, June 17 6:55am Morning Services 7:30am Amud Yomi 2:00pm Mincha at 1818 Market or Mikveh Israel 6:45pm Mincha 7:00pm Wine Tasting with Yaakov Zimmerman followed by Maariv Thursday, June 18 6:45am Morning Services 7:30am Amud Yomi 2:00pm Mincha at 1818 Market or Mikveh Israel 8:15pm Mincha/Maariv* Friday, June 19 6:55am Morning Services 7:30am Amud Yomi 7:00pm Mincha/Kabbalat Shabbat/Maariv 8:14pm Candle Lighting *At this time, Weekday Mincha/Ma'ariv will only be held if at least 10 people sign up in advance. Please email the shul to RSVP for a particular service or to be added to our Minyan WhatsApp Group. ** Please note that Friday Night Services will remain at 7:00pm through the Summer. We all benefit from the wonderful work done by Mekor volunteers! Please consider helping out with the following initiatives: Join the Kiddush (setup) Committee Join the Hospitality Committee Make cholent for Kiddush. Erik will teach you how to make the special Mekor Habracha recipe! Please contact Erik Schneiman @ erik.schneiman@gmail.com Be a Mashgiach for IKC establishments Be an Eruv Checker Training will be provided for all positions. issues.title: The Eruv is Back Up!
issues.searchText: Eruv Update After an enormous amount of work and coordination, we are happy to report that the Eruv is up for Shabbat! A huge thank you to Rabbi Gore, who oversees Eruv repairs and maintenance and was out working until midnight on Thursday night to make the necessary repairs. Thank you as well to Rabbanit Fruchter, who helped connect us with the right people so access could be granted to the affected areas and the needed work could be completed. Please use this opportunity to support the Eruv. We truly need the support of the community in order to maintain and improve an Eruv that serves everyone. You can donate here. Shabbat shalom, Rabbi Eliezer Hirsch Director, Center City Eruv Copyright © 2026 Mekor Habracha, All rights reserved.
